meaning: n.污迹,熏烟v.弄脏,用浓烟熏,染污
examples: There was a dark smudge on his forehead.
She kissed me, careful not to smudge me with her fresh lipstick.
You have a smudge of chocolate on your shirt.
Still, it makes sense to smudge the line between ordinary furniture and the tech-centric kind.
Furthermore, with anti-smudge technology, those greasy thumb prints are a thing of the past.
